project.title: Conserved phosphorylation mechanism regulates the interaction between the Cdc45-MCM2-7-GINS (CMG) replicative helicase complex and its forked DNA substrate
project.projectDescription: Mapping of the Chk1 phosphorylation sites in Drosophila CMG in vitro with recombinant proteins
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The kinase reactions were carried out with 1 mM [18O]ATP in the following buffer: 25 mM Hepes-NaOH pH 7.6, 100 mM sodium acetate, 10 mM magnesium acetate, 10 % glycerol, 1 mM DTT. LysC and trypsin digestion of phosphorylated proteins and subsequent sample preparation for LC-MS/MS analysis were performed as described previously (Piirsoo, A., Pink, A., Kasak, L., Kala, M., Kasvandik, S., Ustav, M. and Piirsoo, M. (2019) Differential phosphorylation determines the repressor and activator potencies of GLI1 proteins and their efficiency in modulating the HPV life cycle. PloS One, 14, e0225775). The 18O labeled phosphorylation sites were mapped in two experiments. In the first analysis (Dm1), the digested peptides were separated on a 15 cm × 75 μm ID emitter column (New Objective, USA) packed with 3 μm ReproSil-Pur C18AQ beads (Dr. Maisch, Germany), attached to an Agilent 1200 series nano-LC. A linear 120 min 8-45% gradient of solvent B at a flow rate of 250 nl/min was used to elute the peptides from the column (buffer A: 0.1% formic acid; buffer B: 80% acetonitrile + 0.1% formic acid). The peptides were detected using an LTQ Orbitrap XL mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Each 300-1800 m/z MS scan at a resolution setting 60 0000 was followed by MS/MS analysis of up to 5 most intense peaks. In the second analysis (Dm2), the peptides were separated using an Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano system (Dionex) equipped with a C18 trap column (Dionex) and the same analytical C18 emitter column as in the first run, using a linear 90 min 8-40% gradient of solvent B at a flow rate of 200 nl/min. The peptides were detected using a Q Exactive Plus mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific).  Each 350-1400 m/z MS scan at a resolution setting of 70 000 was followed by MS/MS analysis of up to 10 most intense ions. Only charge states over +1 were analyzed in both experiments.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw MS data files were processed using the MaxQuant software package version 2.2.0.0. Phospho 18O (STY) and phospho (STY) modifications were set as variable modifications, and the default parameters were used for identification. Peptide identification searches were performed against the compiled subset of the appropriate kinase and substrate subunit reference protein sequences downloaded in September 2023 from the UniProtKB database (https://www.uniprot.org/).
